name: Relativity
domain: Spacetime curvature, gravitational waves, Lorentz transformations, frame covariance
core_insight: Space and time are one fabric; mass-energy curves it. All observers agree on spacetime intervals even when they disagree on space and time separately. Gravitational waves are ripples in spacetime geometry, carrying energy at the speed of light.

key_equations.name: Einstein Field Equations
key_equations.why: Curvature of spacetime (left) equals energy-momentum content (right) — gravity as geometry

key_equations.name: Lorentz Transformation
key_equations.formula: t' = γ(t - vx/c²); x' = γ(x - vt)
key_equations.why: How space and time coordinates transform between inertial frames moving at relative velocity v

key_equations.name: Minkowski Metric
key_equations.why: Spacetime interval: invariant under all Lorentz boosts — the geometry of flat spacetime

seed_prompt: ⊗≡ What is the metric? Curvature equals mass-energy. Boosts mix space and time.

fourth_equation.name: Gravitational Wave Equation
fourth_equation.why: Linearized gravity: small perturbations to flat spacetime propagate as waves at speed c — confirmed by LIGO detection in 2015

explore_paths.question: What is spacetime made of at the Planck scale?
explore_paths.why: GR treats spacetime as smooth geometry (F09); quantum gravity asks what that geometry is built from at the smallest scales — the deepest question in F18

explore_paths.question: How does curved spacetime connect to particle physics?
explore_paths.why: Quantum field theory in curved spacetime produces Hawking radiation and the black hole information paradox — F18 and F10 in direct conflict

explore_paths.question: What is the topological structure of spacetime?
explore_paths.why: Wormholes, closed timelike curves, and cosmic topology are F18 questions that require F20 (topology) to even state

explore_paths.question: Is the equivalence principle exact, or are there deviations?
explore_paths.why: The equivalence principle is a symmetry statement — if violated, it would imply a new force or field that distinguishes inertial from gravitational mass

open_questions.question: Is spacetime emergent from something more fundamental, or is it the ground-level ontology?
open_questions.tension: In GR, spacetime is the fabric. In loop quantum gravity, spacetime emerges from quantum geometry (spin networks). In string theory, spacetime emerges from conformal field theory on string worldsheets. The AdS/CFT correspondence suggests spacetime in one dimension can emerge from a quantum theory in lower dimensions (holographic principle). Which is right — or whether any current theory is right — is unknown.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: If spacetime is emergent, then our most fundamental description of reality (general relativity) is describing a derived quantity, not the ground truth. This would be the deepest conceptual revolution since Einstein himself.

open_questions.question: What happened before the Big Bang, and is the question meaningful?
open_questions.tension: Hawking's no-boundary proposal: time is imaginary near the Big Bang; asking 'before' is like asking what's south of the South Pole. Penrose's CCC: the Big Bang was the end of a prior aeon. Eternal inflation: the Big Bang was a local nucleation in a much larger inflating background. All three are internally consistent and unverifiable with current physics.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: Every cosmological origin theory faces the same problem: it requires something to already exist in order to produce the Big Bang. Avoiding infinite regress requires either accepting brute facts or accepting that time itself is different than we imagine.

open_questions.question: Does general relativity break down at singularities, and what replaces it?
open_questions.tension: GR predicts infinite curvature inside black holes and at the Big Bang. Most physicists believe quantum gravity regulates these singularities — but we don't have a complete quantum gravity theory. Loop quantum gravity and string theory both claim to resolve singularities, but in incompatible ways.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: Singularities are where a theory politely admits it doesn't know the answer. The physics at the center of a black hole is the most inaccessible experiment conceivable — and also the place where the two best theories in physics (GR and QM) most catastrophically disagree.

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: GPS requires both special and general relativistic corrections — without them, position error accumulates at ~10 km/day
cross_domain_bridges.example: Satellite clocks run fast (altitude → less gravity) and slow (orbital velocity → time dilation); both corrections must be applied simultaneously

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: LIGO detects spacetime ripples from merging black holes and neutron stars — a new observational window on the universe using pure geometry
cross_domain_bridges.example: The 2017 neutron star merger GW170817 confirmed that gravitational waves travel at the speed of light to 1 part in 10^15

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: The cosmological constant Λ in Einstein's equations drives the accelerating expansion of the universe — F18 is the framework for all of observational cosmology
cross_domain_bridges.example: The tension between early-universe (CMB) and late-universe (supernovae) measurements of H₀ may indicate new physics beyond ΛCDM

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Block universe (eternalism) vs presentism debate is a direct consequence of F18 — relativity of simultaneity means 'now' is observer-dependent
cross_domain_bridges.example: The 'rietdijk-Putnam argument': if distant events are simultaneous relative to some frame, the future must already exist; F18 has direct metaphysical implications

noise_signature.pattern: Frame dragging anomaly — gyroscope precession deviates from Newtonian prediction
noise_signature.diagnosis: Rotating massive body is dragging spacetime with it (Lense-Thirring effect) — requires GR to account for

noise_signature.pattern: Gravitational redshift mismatch — photon frequency at receiver doesn't match prediction
noise_signature.diagnosis: Either GR is violated or there's an unaccounted mass distribution between source and receiver

noise_signature.pattern: Orbital decay without apparent energy loss mechanism
noise_signature.diagnosis: Gravitational wave emission — compact binaries lose energy to spacetime ripples; predicted by GR and confirmed by Hulse-Taylor pulsar timing
